mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer spending, as measured by RSAFS, signals robust aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: economic expansion with growing consumer confidence
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 60-day returns is non-positive, or the signal fails t-test for positive median returns over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, the Fed is highly accommodative, sharply reducing discount rates and encouraging risk-taking, which reliably lifts S&P 500 returns over the following week.
regime_hint: zero lower bound or near-zero rate environment
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, median 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong consumer demand (RSAFS above 600,000) and very accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS below 1%) create a uniquely favorable macro combination that boosts both earnings and valuations, driving strong positive S&P 500 returns within a month.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery or late-cycle boom with low rates
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the joint signal fails to produce a median 20-day return significantly above the unconditional median (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5% and has been stable or declining, the labor market is tight, supporting wage growth and consumer spending, which feeds into corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: low and stable unemployment regime (labor market tight)
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 5%, the forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and overall economic activity, which directly increases corporate earnings and supports higher equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The 5-year rolling rank correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day returns is negative or not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL growth) exceeds 0.3%, it signals rising inflationary pressures that prompt expectations of monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
regime_hint: inflation scare or early tightening phase
failure_condition: After the trigger, 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong payroll gains (PAYEMS growth above 200,000 jobs per month) and robust housing starts (HOUST above 1,400,000) jointly signal a self-reinforcing economic expansion where rising employment supports housing demand and construction activity, boosting overall corporate profits and equity returns within a month.
regime_hint: broad-based expansion with synchronized labor and housing strength
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the median 20-day forward return is not significantly above the unconditional median (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a sustained tightening cycle where the federal funds rate rises for three consecutive months, increasing discount rates progressively reduce the present value of future cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS higher than previous month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, the median forward 60-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the rank IC over any 2-year window is non-negative.
